other_observers: Susan Allerton, John Bregar, Jim Beatty, Barb Byron, Heather Morris; Riley Morris
description: Completely rufous colored head except for white colored strip running from base of bill up forehead. Bill was a very pale blue. Eye appeared dark. Pale rosy colored breast. Sides were grey and so were the scapulars. The tertials and primaries looked black. White hip patch was apparent. Uppertail coverts and undertail coverts appeared black and tail itself was black. Bird was identical in size to American Wigeons which were swimming around it. Unable to observe bird in flight.Behaviors: The bird was feeding most of the time it was being observed. This involved the bird putting its head underwater and its tail straight up in the air. The l It was feeding among many other ducks including many American Wigeons, Northern Shovelers, and Mallards. When not feeding the bird was swimming among other ducks.Call: Unable to hear due to distance and so many other birds vocalizingPlumage: Adult Breeding
